question: Even out of the factory, no two cells are identical, but we need to catch the outliers fast. Using our accelerated testbed data, establish a baseline degradation curve for a standard cell batch. Then, run an anomaly detection prediction to identify which cells are degrading significantly faster than the baseline due to intrinsic manufacturing variability. Flag the top 5% of cells that deviate from the standard State-of-Life (SOL) curve early in their cycle life. Show me the divergence graphs and the error margins. I need to prove to the manufacturing floor that we can spot defective cells within the first 50 cycles.
